results.title: Overtone focusing in biphonic Tuvan throat singing (eLife)
results.judge_reason: Direct source-domain match: this paper explicitly describes biphonic Tuvan khoomei with F2/F3 merging in vocal tract producing the focused filter peak — the exact source mechanism in the candidate.

results.title: Tuvan throat singing — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: General Wikipedia overview of Tuvan throat singing covers the biphonic fundamental+overtone mechanism that is the candidate's source domain.

results.title: Overtone focusing in biphonic Tuvan throat singing (bioRxiv)
results.judge_reason: Preprint companion to the eLife paper — same source-domain mechanism (F2/F3 merging, focused filter peak in biphonic khoomei) as candidate.

results.title: Squeezing-Heads Distillation (2025/2)
keyword_overlap_terms: head merging (via merging multiple teacher heads into fewer student heads)
results.judge_reason: Conceptually related — squeezes multiple heads into fewer, similar in spirit to head-merging, but the candidate's distinctive 'biphonic dual-head decoder with one fundamental + one selected overtone' is not implemented; SHD targets distillation compression rather than parallel biphonic output.

results.title: MoH: Mixture-of-Head Attention (2025/12)
results.judge_reason: MoH addresses head specialization/sparsity but does not implement the candidate's specific dual-output biphonic decoding with formant-style merging of two heads to produce a sharp single overtone.

results.title: Fast KV Compaction via Attention Matching (2026/2)
results.judge_reason: KV-cache compaction with per-head ratios is orthogonal to biphonic dual-decoder-head selective-overtone amplification; no meaningful overlap.

results.title: Dynamically Composable Multi-Head Attention (2024/5)
results.judge_reason: Composes heads dynamically but does not target a biphonic dual-decoder output architecture or salient-datum extraction at a sharp filter peak as in candidate.

results.title: TransMLA: Multi-Head Latent Attention (2025/2)
results.judge_reason: PCA-based latent head reduction is unrelated to the candidate's parallel dual-decoder biphonic concept with selective overtone amplification.

summary.fail_reason: Three direct source-domain matches (eLife, Wikipedia, bioRxiv) on Tuvan khoomei biphonic singing with F2/F3 formant merging — the candidate's source mechanism is already explicitly documented in prior work, so the source half is not novel.
